Ntc Of Mtn To Compel, Response To Request For Documents And Inposing Monetary Sanctions; Declartation; Memo Of P And A; Response To Interrogatories; Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
1. Motion for order compelling response to request for production of documents; and imposing monetary sanctions granted;
a. All objections are waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within ten days of the date of service of this order, defendant Social Betterment Development Company shall serve full, complete and verified response to plaintiff's request for production of documents;
c. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00 to run against defendant;
2. Motion for order compelling response to interrogatories and imposing monetary sanctions granted;
a. All objections waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within ten days of the date of service of this order, defendant Social Betterment Development Company shall serve full, complete and verified response to plaintiff's special interrogatories (first set);
c. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $250.00 to run against defendant;
3. Motion for order that matters in request for admissions be deemed admitted and imposing monetary sanctions granted in part, denied in part:
a. The matters set forth in plaintiff's request for admissions (set one) are deemed admitted;
b. Request for sanctions denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
